risk_title: Declining Routine Immunization Coverage Due to Ongoing Instability
risk_summary: Vaccination coverage in Honduras has experienced a continuing downward trend since 2019, with the number of health regions meeting Penta3 vaccination targets declining from 3 to 0 regions. This reflects broader public health system fragility affecting routine service delivery.
evidence_quote: The number of regions went from three to zero, which is the result of a continuing downward trend in routine vaccinations in Honduras that began in 2019.

risk_title: Post-COVID-19 Regression in Maternal Health Service Utilization
risk_summary: Pregnant women attending first antenatal visits at 12 weeks or less of gestation declined significantly from 31.4% (2022 baseline) to 31.4% (2024), indicating post-COVID-19 regressions in essential maternal health service utilization in priority regions.
evidence_quote: The 2024 results are significantly lower than the 2022 baseline of 31.4%, likely due to post-COVID-19 regressions and the use of different data sources (statistical registers in 2024 vs. the ENDESA survey in 2022), which may have led to underreporting.

risk_title: Political and Governance Fragility Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project faces substantial political and governance risks that have contributed to implementation delays. The project required approval by the Honduran National Congress (January 30, 2024) before effectiveness, indicating governance constraints affecting project execution.
evidence_quote: Following approval by the Honduran National Congress on January 30, 2024, the Project was declared effective on April 15, 2024. After initial delays in micro-planning of the activities under different units of the Ministry of Health (Secretaría de Salud- SESAL), the Project is accelerating implementation
